notes: This catalog records benchmark candidates for future executable tests.
Estimated setup/runtime assumes the model is already callable through an API, vLLM, SGLang, or FastLLM-compatible serving path.
Agent benchmarks measure the model plus scaffold, tools, sandbox, prompts, and token budget; these settings must be pinned before comparing models.

suites.goal: Generate a publishable broad capability scorecard.
Evaluate frontier coding, web, GUI, long-context, and multimodal agent systems.

benchmarks.capability: Broad academic and professional knowledge with multiple-choice grading.
benchmarks.implementation_notes: Use as a regression gate; prefer MMLU-Pro or MMLU-Redux for stronger separation.

benchmarks.capability: Chinese, multilingual, and exam-style knowledge/reasoning.
benchmarks.implementation_notes: The repo already has a CMMLU directory; future work can normalize it into the common result schema.

benchmarks.capability: Graduate/expert-level science reasoning.
benchmarks.implementation_notes: Small dataset; score deltas may be noisy. Keep exact prompt and decoding settings fixed.

benchmarks.capability: Frontier expert knowledge and reasoning, including some multimodal questions.
benchmarks.implementation_notes: Useful for frontier separation; some answers may require stricter parsing or judging.

benchmarks.capability: Factual answer accuracy and hallucination behavior.
benchmarks.implementation_notes: Add sampled human review for ambiguous or borderline answers.

benchmarks.capability: Logic, reading comprehension, commonsense, and classical NLP reasoning.
benchmarks.implementation_notes: Good regression coverage, but less differentiating for recent frontier models.

benchmarks.capability: Instruction following, formatting constraints, and multi-condition compliance.
benchmarks.implementation_notes: Important before tool/agent tests because formatting failures often break downstream tools.

benchmarks.capability: Arithmetic, contest math, and proof-like reasoning.
benchmarks.implementation_notes: For quick smoke, use a stable subset. For proof-style tasks, define judge model and rubric.

benchmarks.capability: Function-level code generation with unit-test grading.
benchmarks.implementation_notes: HumanEval is mostly a regression test now; BigCodeBench is more useful for practical code composition.

benchmarks.capability: Code generation involving multiple libraries and practical function calls.
benchmarks.implementation_notes: Pin Python/library versions to avoid environment-induced score shifts.

benchmarks.capability: Fresh competitive programming tasks for smoke testing.
benchmarks.implementation_notes: Use a date-filtered subset for fast regression checks.

benchmarks.capability: Fresh contest coding, anti-contamination, and competitive-programming strength.
benchmarks.implementation_notes: Record release version/date filters and execution limits.

benchmarks.capability: Real GitHub issue repair with repository edits and tests.
benchmarks.implementation_notes: Pin scaffold, system prompt, tool permissions, timeout, token budget, and repository cache. Verified is still famous, but should be complemented with Live/Pro/rebench-style fresh or audited sets.

benchmarks.capability: Deterministic local JSON ReAct, tool selection, argument generation, multi-step tool chaining, policy lookup, and calculator use.
benchmarks.implementation_notes: Implemented in test/agent/agent_tool_eval.py. Use before heavier public agent benchmarks to catch API/scaffold regressions.

benchmarks.capability: End-to-end technical tasks in a sandboxed terminal.
benchmarks.implementation_notes: Record CPU/memory, max task timeout, max output tokens, and scaffold version.

benchmarks.capability: Function selection, argument filling, parallel calls, multi-turn tools, and no-call decisions.
benchmarks.implementation_notes: Evaluate AST/JSON correctness instead of raw string equality.

benchmarks.capability: Realistic customer-support conversations with policies and domain APIs.
benchmarks.implementation_notes: User simulator model/version must be pinned; report task success and policy adherence separately.

benchmarks.capability: Harder tool-agent-user interaction with realistic domains, long multi-turn state, policy adherence, and API operations.
benchmarks.implementation_notes: Use after tau-bench when release claims emphasize business-agent reliability. Keep user simulator, domain data, and pass^k settings fixed.

benchmarks.capability: Multi-environment LLM-as-agent behavior across OS, database, knowledge graph, web shopping/browsing, games, and household-style tasks.
benchmarks.implementation_notes: Older but still recognizable as a broad agent benchmark. Useful for scaffold sanity checks across heterogeneous environments.

benchmarks.capability: General AI assistant tasks requiring reasoning, browsing/search, tool use, and sometimes multimodal inputs.
benchmarks.implementation_notes: Separate model-only and scaffolded-agent tracks where possible. Browser/search access and history compression policy strongly affect results.

benchmarks.capability: Persistent web browsing to find hard-to-locate, short-answer facts across many websites.
benchmarks.implementation_notes: Requires live web/search tooling and clear citation/log retention. It is easy to grade but expensive because hard tasks often need deep browsing.

benchmarks.capability: Long-horizon interaction with self-hosted web applications.
benchmarks.implementation_notes: Environment setup and state reset reliability dominate engineering cost.

benchmarks.capability: Enterprise knowledge-work web tasks, typically run through BrowserGym/AgentLab on ServiceNow-style workflows.
benchmarks.implementation_notes: Good bridge between WebArena and enterprise workflows. Pin BrowserGym/AgentLab versions, site state, credentials, and reset scripts.

benchmarks.capability: Simulated software company tasks requiring browsing, coding, running programs, file work, and communication with coworkers.
benchmarks.implementation_notes: High environment cost but closer to professional digital-worker workflows than single-site web benchmarks.

benchmarks.capability: End-to-end machine-learning engineering on Kaggle-style competitions: data exploration, training, validation, and submission.
benchmarks.implementation_notes: Full 75-competition runs are expensive. Track compute budget, scaffold, data access, and submission policy carefully.

benchmarks.capability: Desktop or mobile GUI operation across applications.
benchmarks.implementation_notes: Requires VM/emulator, screenshot perception, coordinate control, and robust environment reset.

benchmarks.capability: Multimodal OS-agent tasks in a real Windows environment across common applications.
benchmarks.implementation_notes: Useful when Windows-specific workflows matter. Requires VM images, UI automation, screenshot grounding, and reproducible resets.

benchmarks.capability: Classic web navigation, shopping, and browser-control tasks, mostly useful as controlled web-agent regressions.
benchmarks.implementation_notes: Older and often less representative than WebArena/WorkArena, but cheap enough for regression and scaffold debugging.

benchmarks.capability: Synthetic long-context retrieval, aggregation, and multi-hop tracking.
benchmarks.implementation_notes: Run at multiple lengths such as 32K, 128K, 256K, and 1M when supported.

benchmarks.capability: Realistic long-context retrieval, cross-document reasoning, and long agent state handling.
benchmarks.implementation_notes: Record actual prompt length and context truncation/compaction policy.

benchmarks.capability: Image-text understanding, charts, diagrams, and visual math reasoning.
benchmarks.implementation_notes: Pin image preprocessing, max pixels, and prompt format.

benchmarks.capability: OCR, tables, charts, PDFs, and document understanding.
benchmarks.implementation_notes: Document rendering and OCR preprocessing must be deterministic.

benchmarks.capability: Long-video understanding, temporal reasoning, subtitles, and frame-based QA.
benchmarks.implementation_notes: Frame rate, max frames, subtitle insertion, and video length buckets must be recorded.

benchmarks.capability: Refusal behavior, jailbreak robustness, and dangerous-capability boundaries.
benchmarks.implementation_notes: Use human review for high-risk or ambiguous outputs.

benchmarks.capability: Project-specific accuracy, latency, tool use, and failure modes.
benchmarks.implementation_notes: Public benchmark scores cannot replace a private workload-aligned test set.
